Title: The genetic architecture of amino acids dissection by association and linkage analysis in maize

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Association panel of 513 diverse inbred lines; BB population (B73/By804); validation involved B73-like and By804-like haplotypes
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for quality protein maize improvement because favorable alleles at the 27-kDa γ-zein locus and the independent 15.26-kb duplication can be combined to improve kernel lysine composition. Best deployed by marker-assisted selection together with o2-modifier breeding; caution that the locus affects storage protein composition and should be combined with agronomically normal backgrounds.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Natural variation at GRMZM2G138727, including a nearby duplication/haplotype, is associated with Lys/Total in maize kernels; the duplication and favorable haplotypes increase expression of the 27-kDa γ-zein locus and significantly alter lysine proportion.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS colocalization within a validated chromosome-7 QTL; heterogeneous inbred family progeny testing; expression-QTL analysis; expression–phenotype correlation; duplication genotyping using the 0707 primer; haplotype analysis.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown self-pollinated mature kernels from multiple environments/populations; no specific treatment factor reported for the gene effect.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in developing maize kernel/endosperm; expression level in 15-DAP kernels was significantly affected by the duplication and haplotype background.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS with 1.25M SNPs; RNA-seq expression data from 15-DAP kernels (368 lines); eQTL analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The validated QTL corresponds to the qγ27/o2 modifier1 region. A 15.26-kb duplication encompassing four genes (GRMZM2G138727, GRMZM2G565441, GRMZM2G138976, GRMZM5G873335) affected both Lys/Total and expression of these genes. BB parents lacked the duplication, implying an additional causal variant. Low LD between the duplication and GRMZM2G138727 haplotype indicates two independent variants; combined effects explained more variance than either alone.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering zein/storage-protein composition may shift endosperm protein balance; no direct yield penalty was reported here.
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: B73/By804-related haplotypes in elite maize germplasm
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A major chromosome-7 Lys/Total QTL was narrowed to a region containing GRMZM2G138727. 2. A previously reported 15.26-kb duplication at the qγ27/o2 modifier1 locus significantly affected Lys/Total and expression of four local genes including GRMZM2G138727. 3. Independent haplotype effects at GRMZM2G138727 also altered Lys/Total, supporting this storage-protein locus as a causal determinant of kernel amino acid composition.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential amino acid for human and animal nutrition; determinant of maize protein quality

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin promoter (constitutive, used for rice overexpression validation)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Association panel of 513 diverse inbred lines; transgenic rice validation in ZhongHua 11
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A tractable engineering target for tuning branched-chain amino acid composition. Can be stacked with upstream regulators such as O2 or with other BCAA-pathway genes, but editing/overexpression should be optimized because flux redistribution may increase valine while decreasing leucine-related proportions.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first common step in the parallel pathway toward valine/leucine and isoleucine biosynthesis, condensing pyruvate-derived substrates to acetolactate/acetohydroxybutyrate intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ALS is associated with Leu/Total in maize kernels, and overexpression in rice significantly changed branched-chain amino acid traits: valine-related traits increased whereas Leu/BCAA, Leu/AT and Leu/Total decreased.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; cis- and trans-eQTL mapping; expression–trait correlation; O2 co-expression/trans-regulation evidence; heterologous overexpression in rice followed by amino acid profiling.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Committed-step enzyme supplying precursor flux to branched-chain amino acids
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: No special treatment; effects assessed in field-grown maize kernels and transgenic rice plants.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in developing maize kernels (15 DAP); expression positively correlated with Leu/Total.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS with 1.25M SNPs; RNA-seq expression data from 15-DAP kernels (368 lines); eQTL analysis; metabolite/amino-acid profiling in maize and transgenic rice
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The lead SNP chr5.S_163943054 lies ~41 kb upstream of ALS. A strong cis-eQTL and an O2 trans-eQTL were detected, implying transcriptional regulation by O2. Although ALS should affect Val/Leu and Ile pathways, the rice overexpression test changed Val, Leu, Ala and Met but not Ile, indicating context-dependent pathway redistribution.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Valine/leucine branch of branched-chain amino acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive overexpression shifted amino acid balance rather than uniformly increasing all BCAAs; leucine-related proportions decreased in transgenic rice while valine-related traits increased.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key/first committed control step in BCAA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: B73 cDNA used for overexpression construct
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS associated ALS with kernel Leu/Total in maize. 2. ALS expression showed both cis-eQTL and O2-linked trans-eQTL, and higher expression correlated with Leu/Total. 3. Overexpression of maize ALS in rice altered branched-chain amino acid composition, validating causal metabolic function.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Branched-chain amino acids (especially leucine and valine)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential amino acids important for nutritional quality and protein composition

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Association panel of 513 diverse inbred lines
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Amino acid metabolism network (candidate biosynthetic/catabolic enzymes identified by GWAS)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: The paper reconstructed a maize kernel amino acid pathway and proposed these enzymes as breeding/engineering candidates for improving kernel amino acid composition. Individual deployment requires separate validation because most were identified statistically rather than functionally tested.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Multiple enzymatic steps across amino acid anabolism/catabolism, including phosphoglycerate dehydrogenase, L-asparaginase, phenylalanine ammonia-lyase, ketol-acid reductoisomerase, nicotianamine aminotransferase, tryptophan synthase, dihydrodipicolinate reductase, glutamate-ammonia ligase, succinate semialdehyde dehydrogenase, isovaleryl-CoA dehydrogenase, tyrosine decarboxylase, isocitrate dehydrogenase, phosphoribosyl-AMP cyclohydrolase, tryptophan transaminase, serine palmitoyltransferase, glycine hydroxymethyltransferase, asparagine synthase, aconitate hydratase, disulphide oxidoreductase, L-threonine 3-dehydrogenase, shikimate kinase, 3-methyl-2-oxobutanoate dehydrogenase, and 3-dehydroquinate synthase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: These genes were significantly associated by GWAS with variation in maize kernel amino acid traits and were used to reconstruct the maize amino acid metabolic network, suggesting causal roles in amino acid biosynthesis/catabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; annotation-based pathway reconstruction; some genes supported by eQTL and expression–phenotype correlation; co-expression network analysis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms target amino acids directly, supplies precursor pools, or contributes to amino acid catabolism/remodeling depending on the specific enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown mature kernels from two association environments; no gene-specific treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Many were expressed in immature kernels (15 DAP) and some had significant eQTLs and trait correlations.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: GRMZM2G081886; GRMZM2G139463; GRMZM2G118345; GRMZM2G161868; GRMZM2G006480; GRMZM2G169593; GRMZM2G090241; GRMZM2G036464; GRMZM2G119482; GRMZM2G381051; GRMZM2G009400; GRMZM5G829778; GRMZM2G082214; GRMZM2G127308; GRMZM2G010202; GRMZM2G004824; GRMZM2G078472; GRMZM2G009808; GRMZM2G091819; GRMZM2G147191; GRMZM2G139412; GRMZM2G037614; GRMZM2G178826
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Name: PGDH; AI/L-asparaginase; PAL; KARI; NNAT; TS; DHDPR; GAL/Glutamate-ammonia ligase; SSADH; IVD; TyrDC; IDH; PRA-CH; TGTA/Tryptophan transaminase; SPT; GHMT; AS; ACO; DSOR; TD; SK; MODH; DHQS
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream to downstream steps across multiple amino acid biosynthetic and catabolic branches
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS with 1.25M SNPs; RNA-seq expression data from 15-DAP kernels; eQTL analysis; co-expression network
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: These 23 genes were highlighted in the Results as pathway candidates from GWAS/network reconstruction, but most were not individually validated beyond association, annotation, and transcript-based support. Five had previous reports in maize (IDH, PAL, TS, AS, ACO).
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Pyruvate-derived, glutamate-derived, aspartate-derived, serine-derived, histidine, and phenylalanine-derived amino acid pathways plus TCA-cycle links
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not individually defined in this paper; modifying central amino acid enzymes may cause broad metabolic pleiotropy.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Enzyme families corresponding to the listed reactions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Twenty-three candidate enzyme genes from GWAS were placed into a reconstructed maize amino acid metabolic network. 2. Several showed eQTL and expression–trait correlations, supporting transcription-linked metabolic effects. 3. These genes define the broader pathway architecture underlying kernel amino acid variation, although most still need direct functional validation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protein building blocks and determinants of nutritional quality

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Association panel of 513 diverse inbred lines
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A major breeding target for quality protein maize. Natural alleles modulating O2 expression can improve lysine composition without the severe agronomic penalties of classical o2 mutants. Best used with modifier loci such as qγ27 and with marker-assisted selection for elite backgrounds.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: O2 expression is negatively correlated with Lys/Total in maize kernels; natural variation at the O2 locus contributes significantly to kernel lysine composition and broader amino acid pathway regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS for Lys/Total; cis-eQTL mapping; expression–phenotype correlation; co-expression analysis; overlap with published ChIP-seq/RNA-seq O2 target data.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major switch-like regulator of maize endosperm protein and amino acid composition, influencing Lys/Total directly and controlling many amino acid-metabolism genes including ALS.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown mature kernels from two environments; expression measured in 15-DAP kernels; no specific treatment.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed during maize kernel development, particularly endosperm; significant cis-eQTL in 15-DAP kernels.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Not explicitly established; acts as an upstream transcriptional regulator of amino acid/storage-protein pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Amino acid biosynthesis network regulation and endosperm storage-protein deposition
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS with 1.25M SNPs; RNA-seq expression data from 15-DAP kernels (368 lines); cis-eQTL analysis; co-expression network; comparison with published ChIP-seq/RNA-seq in o2 mutant
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The lead SNP chr7.S_10695002 lies ~98 kb downstream of O2 and was associated with both Lys/Total and O2 expression. O2 is already known as a bZIP regulator of endosperm zein genes, and this study extends its role to broad amino acid pathway control. The materials analyzed were elite lines with normal field performance, suggesting useful natural O2 variation beyond severe mutant alleles.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Classical o2 mutants increase lysine but often show poor agronomic performance; therefore allele choice and combination with modifiers are essential.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ALS/GRMZM2G143008; 22-kD α-zein genes; β-zein genes; broader amino acid-metabolism gene set identified by co-expression and prior ChIP/RNA data
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation/regulation of storage-protein and amino-acid-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Regulates zein genes and positively influences ALS expression; higher O2 expression is associated with altered amino acid network output and lower Lys/Total.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O2 was identified by GWAS/eQTL as a major locus for kernel Lys/Total. 2. O2 expression showed a strong cis-eQTL and was significantly negatively correlated with lysine proportion. 3. Co-expression and prior target data indicate O2 is a central regulator linking endosperm storage proteins and amino acid biosynthesis, including trans-regulation of ALS.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential amino acid for food/feed quality
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Kernel developmental program; natural cis-regulatory variation at the O2 locus
